title: CSS Positioning Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential properties for controlling the placement and layout of elements on your page.

questions.question: Which of these is the DEFAULT positioning value for every HTML element?
Which positioning value removes an element from the normal document flow and positions it relative to its closest positioned ancestor?
Which positioning value keeps an element in the same place on the screen even when the user scrolls the page?
Which positioning value behaves like 'relative' until the user reaches a specific threshold (e.g., top: 0), then 'sticks' to the screen?
Which property controls the 'stacking order' (which element is on top) when elements overlap?
